Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious
Origin and meaning
According to Richard M. Sherman, co-writer of the song with his brother, Robert, the word
was created by them in two weeks, mostly out of double-talk.
The roots of the word have been defined as follows: super- “above”, cali- “beauty”,
fragilistic- “delicate”, expiali- “to atone”, and docious- “educable”, with the sum of these
parts signifying roughly “Atoning for educability through delicate beauty.” Although the
word contains recognizable English morphemes, it does not follow the rules of English
morphology as a whole. The morphemestic is a suffix in English, whereas the morpheme
ex- is typically a prefix; so following normal English morphological rules, it would represent
two words: supercalifragilistic and expialidocious. The pronunciation also leans towards
it being two words since the letter c does not usually sound like a k when followed by an e.
According to the film, it is defined as “something to say when you have nothing to say”.
(Source: Wikipedia)

The tzion of Harav Hakadosh Elimelech M’lizhensk ZT”L
Several years ago, upon the yahrtzeit of Harav Elimelech from Lizensk, the holy Noam
Elimelech, Avraham Tzvi Klein’s Rebbi in Yeshiva Sh’or Yoshuv taught his Shiur a tefillah that
Reb Elimelech wrote. The Tefillah touched Avraham Tzvi, inspiring this Nigun.
רתויו רתוי ךילא דימת קקותשנשו ךב םיקבד היהנש ונילע ךשדק חור עיפשתו
And You (Hashem) should pour on us Your holy spirit so that we should be attached to
You. And so that we should yearn for You always more and more.
*(from composer Avraham Tzvi Klein)
I asked Rabbi Fischer, “Can you please translate those words for me again?” He did and I
said, “Wow, I have to compose a nigun to these words”. I asked Rabbi Fischer which part of
the words he thought the low part should be and which part of the words the high part should
be. I took his advice and sat at the table in the Bais Medrash to compose the nigun while the
other shiur had some kichel and herring in another room. It was pretty intense. I kind of new
right away that it was B”H a good one but I didn’t think it would catch on in Yeshiva the way
it did and beyond, and that people would be so inspired from it. Special thank you to Rabbi
Yonasan Fischer!

The Shmuz is a 45 minute Torah lecture that focuses on major issues of life.
Issues ranging from “working on anger” to “learning to be a better spouse”
-- from “understanding the reason behind davening” to “what my purpose is
in this world”. So many of concepts that we take as given - yet somehow we
may not have focused on of late.

We’re so honored that you’ve purchased our new album. We love what we do and we’re very
proud of this latest project. We hope you’ll enjoy it over and over again, whether it’s during s’firah
or any other time of year. Thank you for celebrating with us! —Six13
They’re driven by a mission to connect Jews around the globe with their heritage through music,
anchored by a strong Jewish identity, and fueled by thumping beatbox, intricate arrangements, and
soulful harmonies. Six13 is a six-man vocal band that brings an unprecedented style of Jewish music
to the stage, with songs ranging from hip-hop dance tracks to rock anthems. The members of the
New York City-based group sound like a full band – while using nothing but their voices.
Six13 has been featured on The Today Show, CBS News, The View, Time magazine, NBC, Fox, CNN,
ABC, Huffington Post, The Jewish Week, Newsday, The NY Daily News, Howard Stern, Cosmo, Elite
Daily, and several local news media outlets and major market radio stations.
Songs from the group’s six award-winning albums have been played all over both Jewish and secular
radio, and been chosen to appear on ten different “best of” compilation albums. The group has over
20,000 CDs in circulation, and are among the top Jewish music artists on iTunes. They have been
chosen from hundreds of groups, both Jewish and secular, as finalists in the casting process for NBC’s
hit prime-time television series The Sing-Off. The group has also become a viral internet sensation,
with over 4 million views on YouTube.
Six13 has made appearances around the world for millions, to rave reviews and standing ovations.
They’ve played Washington D.C.’s Kennedy Center, traversed New York’s Fifth Avenue in the Salute
to Israel Parade, headlined the North American Jewish Choral Festival three times, and performed
seven times for major league sporting events at Shea Stadium, Citi Field, Madison Square Garden and
Dolphin Stadium. Each year, the group visits dozens of Jewish communities, providing entertain-
ment appropriate for Shabbat or any other day of the week at congregations, religious schools, JCCs,
fundraising events B’nai Mitzvah and private affairs alike.
Call (888) BOOK-613 or e-mail six13@six13.com for more information and to
find out how Six13 can make your concert, celebration or event unforgettable.
Six13 on Facebook: facebook.com/Six13Sings
Six13 on Twitter: @Six13Sings

We’re so honored that you’ve purchased our new album. We love what we do and we’re very proud of this latest project. We
hope you’ll enjoy it over and over again, whether it’s during s’firah or any other time of year. Thank you for believing in us!
—Six13
Fueled by a strong, Jewish identity and anchored by thumping beatbox, intricate arrangements, and soulful harmonies, Six13 is
a six-man vocal band that brings an unprecedented style of Jewish music to the stage. With songs ranging from hip-hop dance
tracks to rock anthems, the members of the New York-based group sound like a full band – while using nothing but their voices.
Six13 has recorded four award-winning albums, whose songs have been played all over Jewish radio and been chosen to appear
on nine different “best of” compilation albums. The group has over 20,000 CDs in circulation, and are among the top Jewish
music artists on iTunes. They have received several distinctions and accolades from Jewish organizations like the National Council
of Young Israel and Moment Magazine, and awards from musical organizations like the Harmony Sweepstakes and the Contem-
porary A Cappella Society of America. They have appeared on the Chabad Telethon, and were chosen from hundreds of groups,
both Jewish and secular, as finalists in the casting process for NBC's hit prime-time television series The Sing-Off. The group has
also become a viral internet sensation, with over one million views on YouTube.
Six13 tours worldwide to rave reviews and standing ovations. Recent highlights include the Salute to Israel Parade, the North
American Jewish Choral Festival, and six appearances at Jewish Heritage Days for major league sports teams, including Shea
Stadium, Citi Field and Madison Square Garden in New York, and Dolphin Stadium in Florida. Each year, the group visits dozens
of Jewish communities, providing entertainment appropriate for Shabbat or any other day of the week at congregations, religious
schools, JCCs, fundraising events, B’nai Mitzvah and private affairs alike.
Visit www.six13.com or call (888) BOOK-613 for more information and to find out how Six13 can make your concert, celebration
or event unforgettable.

albums.full_text:
j.music booklet archive
pdfcd17@gmail.com
Dear friends,
With a thank you to Hashem on my lips and a song of joy in my heart,
I’m happy to greet you once again from the pages of a new CD.
Inspiration. Oh, we all need inspiration. Now more than ever.
Music has the power to inspire.
I hope you will be inspired and uplifted by the songs and messages
contained herein. Let us raise our voices and together ask Hashem:
Bring The House Down! The everlasting “house”, the Beis Hamikdosh,
with the coming of Moshiach speedily in our days. Amein.
Avraham
Lag B’Omer 5776

,ונבהאש םירישה The songs that we loved,
.םינשה לע הדות Thank you for all these years.
,יתוא םיוולמ בצמ לכב יתא םתא You are with me no matter what, you accompany me,
,יתא ךלוהש רוא וישכע םכבל רואב טיבמ I see the light in your hearts, a light that goes with me,
,החמש םכל תתל חוכ יל םינתונ םתא יכ For you give me strength to give you joy,
.החוורל חותפל ילא םכבל תא To open your hearts wide to me.
...ונשגפנש בוט המכ It’s so great to meet each other...
,םירישה תא םיפסוא םידרפנש ינפל עגר Just before we part, we gather the songs,
,םירשואמ םימי ןאכל איבי דועש ןמזל For the time, that will yet bring us happy days,
,םיריהב םיימש ריאי ונילע שדח םוי A new day will shine upon us, bright skies,
.םירישה לכ תא רישנ דוע דחיבו And together, we will sing all the songs.

וניבוהא ,ונידידיו ונידהוא ,ונמולש ישנא לכל הכרבבו הדותב ,הזה תיבה ירעשב האובנ
וחקלו ,הבידנ חורבו הפי ןיעב ,הצפח שפנבו בלב ועייסו וצמאתהש ,ויחיש ונידבוכמו
,תודיסחלו הרותל רוא ילדגמ ,שרדמה תיבו הרות דומלתה ינינב תמקהב הלחנו קלח
.םידסח תולימגו הדובע הרותל הניכשה תארשה לש םוקמ
,קוזיחו דודיע ירבדב ,שעמבו הצעב ,שדוקה תכאלמל סייגתהל וכז דחאכ םלוכ
ונלש .םולה ונעגה םתוכזב קר רשא ,םימוצעו םיהובג םימוכסב ,שדוק תומורתב
אבה םלועל תמייק ןרקה ,םיחצנ חצנלו דעל דומעת םתוכז .אוה םהלש - םכלשו
.הזה םלועב םהיתוריפו
דעוו ירבח ,שדוקה תכאלמל םינמאנ םיפתוש ,םיבושחה ונידידיל תדחוימ הכרב
םשגב ,הלילו םמוי ורסמתהש ,תודסומה דרשמ תווצו ,ןינבה ןרקו תודסומה תלהנה
,יפויהו ראפה תילכתב ,רמג ידיל לודגה תיבה תא איבהל ,םתלוכי יפכמ הלעמל ,חורבו
.הליהתלו תראפתלו םשל
,א"טילש סארג ףסוי ןרהא 'ר ח"הרה ,הזה לודגה תיבה הנוב ,ןמאנה ונדידי ,םלוכ לעו
אלש המוצע תוריסמב ,תוחפטה דעו דסמה ןמ שדוקה תכאלמב רמשמה לע דמעש
דצ לע ,םיאנבה ןינע יפכ ןקותמו האנ ןינב ודי תחת איצוהל הכז ןכאו ,סרפ לבקל תנמ לע
.הלודג הבחרהב ותרותלו 'הל םיתבה תא ךונחל םיכוז ונא ותוכזב ,בוט רתויה
עיקשהש ,א"טילש שילאק דוד 'ר ח"הרה ראופמה ונדידי תא ךרבי הרמז ירישב רחובה
םינוגינה ויהיש ,םייעוצקמהו םיכורבה ויתונורשכ בטימ ,המשנהו בלה תא הז טילקתב
םייוארה שדוק ירישל ןוכנכו יוארכ ,תודיסחה ךרדב השודקד תובהלתהו תומימח יאלמ
יתבבו תודיסחו הרות ילכיהב ,דחי םסנכתהב השעמ ישנאו םידיסח לש םנחלוש לע תולעל
.ול ןח ןח תואושת .'ה לא ברקתהלו קזחתהל ,םמורתהל ךכ ידי לע וכזי ה"זעבו ,לארשי
רעיירב יבצ ףסוי 'ר ג"הרה םסרופמה ךנחמה תורימז םיענ דובכל תדחוימ הכרב
.דחוימה ולוקב םיענהו ,הז טילקתב קלח חקלש ,א"טילש
ןייחלו בטל ,םימיה לכ םתולאשמ לכ יולימב ,בוטה םלומגכ םהל םלשי הרותה ןתונ
,וידחי הל הרבוחש ריעכ היונבה םילשוריב ולת לע הז ןינב דומעיש הכזנ ידע ,םלשלו
.ןמא ונימיב הרהמב קדצ לאוג תאיבב

,יבִּרְקִ ךְוֹתבְּ יחִוּר חַכֹּ המַוּ ,תוֹשׂ עֲלַ ןוֹשׁלָּהַוְ בלֵּהַ לכַוּי רשֶׁאֲ הזֶ המַ
.יבִּ הַּ-וֹלאֱ תמַשְׁנִ היֶהְתִּ דוֹעבְּ ךָדְוֹא ןכֵּ לעַ ,שׁוֹנאֱ תרַמְזִ בטַיתִ ךָלְ הנֵּהִ
)ע"יז לוריבג ןבא המלש יבר ק"הגה תליפת ךותמ(
הזֶ" רשאכ ,ונבל תחמש םוי ,הזה ןמזל ונעיגהו ונמיקו ונייחהש ,בוטה 'הל החמשנו הננרנ "תיִבַּהַ תכַּנֻחֲ רישִׁ רוֹמזְמִ"
תיבו תדה יקיזחמ הרות דומלת ,'הל םילודגה םיתבה ירעש תא חותפלו ךונחל ,"וֹב החָמְשְׂ נִוְ הלָיגִנָ 'ה השָׂ עָ םוֹיּהַ
.הפורצ תודיסחלו האריל ,'ה תדובעלו הרותל רוא-ילדגמ ,ו"צי הריבה ןודנול ונריעב אזלעב ידיסחד יזכרמה שרדמה
ישגר ,המינפ ונבל קמועב רשא תא ועיביש םילימ ןיאו הימוד םלאנ הפה ,תושעל ןושלהו בלה לכוי אל ,הז לודג םויב
תרמז קר ."ןיָנְבִּהַ הזֶ םלֵשְׁהַ" :ונתלאשמ אלימש "ןיָנְקִבְּ םלָוֹע ארֵוֹבּ"ל ונא םישחש בוטה תרכה ישחרו האדוהה
.םיכלמה יכלמ ךלמל המוצע האדוהב ,בלה ךותמ םיאצויו םיעקובה םישגרהה תא איצוהל בטית שונא
,ונא םיאור ןיעב ןיע יכ ,"ךָּדֶוֹא םלָוֹעלְ יקַֹלאֱ 'ה ...ינִתָילִּדִ יכִּ 'ה ךָמְמִוֹראֲ" :"וֹשׁדְקָ רכֶזֵלְ וּדוֹהוְ וידָיסִחֲ 'הלַ וּרמְּזַ" תאזכ תעב
ונמולש ישנא רוביצ תוכזב קר ."ליִחָ תוֹשׂ עֲלַ חַֹכּ ךָלְ ןתֵֹנּהַ אוּה" אלא ,הזה ליחה תא ונל ושע ונידי םצועו ונחוכ אל יכ
,שא תויח ונדעב וצילה רשוי רשאכ ,וז תרקי תניפ תמקהב םירקיה םיפתושה ,י"עה םיצרמנה םינקסעה לכו ויחיש
חולצלו רובעל ונל ודמעש ןה – א"טילש ר"ומדא ןרמ ק"כ לש תושודקה ויתוכרבו ,ונילע ןגי םתוכז םישודקה וניתובר
."ןמֵאָ רמַאֹנוְ ,המָלֵשְׁ הכָרָבְבִּ דחַיַ וּנלָּכֻּ וּנתָוֹא ךְרֵבָיְ ןכֵּ ...וּניתֵוֹבאֲ וּכרְבָּתְנִּשֶׁ וֹמכְּ :'ה תאמ וניתשקב ןכ לעו .השקה ךרדה תא
בוטה םבל ,הבהא ףוצר וכות ,לכה לעמ ךא .תועמדו עגי ,עזי ,םד דצל ,וללה םינינבב ועקשוה םימוצע ףסכ ימוכס
תמקה ןעמל ,הידמולו השודקה הרותה ןעמל קר ושע תאז לכ אלה .םימלוע תלחנו קלח םהב ולטנש ולא לכ לש

.ללפתהל המ
.ידמע התא יכ יל בוטש המו יל רסחש המ עדוי ירה התא
וליאכ יתשקב וליאכ יתליפת ךינפל הצורמ אהי םלוע לש ונוביר

התוא רמול וגהנש תוליהק ויה .ל"צז י'גאלפ םייח יבר ןואגל "םייחה קיפסה אל שיאו רתויב ורהימ םתאצבו ,ע"יז ךלמילא 'ר יברה ק"הרה
לש ורקובב וא שודקה םוי ברעב הנשב תחא הורמאש ויהו ,רקוב לכב .םהמע רבדלו םהינפ תא תוארל
אלוליהד אמויב םע ברב הרמאל ודיפקהש םיקידצ ויהו ,םירופיכה םוי :ןושלה הזב ע"יז ק"הרה םהל בישהו ,הזמ ע"יז ק"הרה ןרמל ולאש רשאכ
לכו ויתולכו ויתונבל הרוה ץישאדארמ אשידק אבסה ק"הרה ןכ ומכ .אבר ןטוקמ תואיכ םתלפתב םינווכמ םניא םלועה בורש יתיאר רשאכ"
המגרתל הרוה ףאו ,דימת וז הבושח הליפת ללפתהל ויאצאצמ םישנה םתעד ןווכל םילוכי םניא הסנרפה ישוקו ןמזה תדרט לדוגמו ,םתגשה

."חרפו רותפכ" יתצעייתהו ,םתחסונמ תחא הלפת וליפא תונשל תאז תושעל אלש יתאמ

TO THE TAUBENFELDS
There are only a handful of people who we meet in our lifetime and touch us in a dramatic way. Izzy ה’’עָּwas one of those people
whom I will never forget. May the inspiration that this album brings to people around the world be תוכזדמלמfor him. May his המשנ
have an הילע.
Benny, Thank you for everything from my first day on the scene until this very day! You are a real mentsch. ה’’בקהshould bentch you
to continue to be חילצמin all of your endeavours.
TO THE COMPOSERS, Elimelech Blumstein, Yossi Green, Eli Laufer, Baruch Levine, Pinny Ostreicher, Elie Schwab, Yitzy Waldner, Yaakov
Ben Shabbat, R' Noach Palay & Moshe Werzberger - It is a true zechus to be able to sing your niggunim.
TO THE ARRANGERS, Ami Cohen, Mendy Hershkowitz, Yisroel Lamm, Pinny Ostreicher, Leib Yaakov Rigler, Moshe Roth -
Your musical talents shone all throughout this project.
TO MY CHASHUVA CHAVRUSA,
Moishy Eider - You're always there to remind me “There's never a break from learning Hashem's Torah”. Thank you!!
MY EXTENDED FAMILY, Gabay, Futerman, Aronov, Louk, Sebbag, Bakshi, and Levy families - Thanks for always rooting me on!
TO SRULY MEYER
My PR man and Graphic Designer. You’re so good at what you do. You always help me see things from a different angle.
TO MY BROTHERS
דַחַי םַּג םיִחָא תֶבֶׁש םיִעָּנ הַמּו בֹוט הַמ הֵּנִה- Lior and Michael. Thanks for your continuous support! You guys are the best!
TO MY PARENTS
IMA, Thank you for your constant encouragement and advice.
ABA, Thanks for showing me your support. Coming to the studio to give your opinion really means the world to me. Thanks!
TO MY CHILDREN
Eliyahu, Ephraim, Akiva, Yosef, and Batsheva Rachel, I love you dearly. You make me so proud. You should grow to become tremendous
sources of Yiddish nachas to your parents and most of all to the Ribono Shel Olam.
TO MY FATHER-IN-LAW,Aba, You should continue to have nachas from your children and grandchildrenהנש םירשעו האמ דע.
TO MY EISHES CHAYIL, SHIRA ש’’ומע
Thank you for always being there for me. All the late nights and long studio hours in order to produce this album couldn't have been
done without your support and sacrifice.
FINALLY, AND MOST OF ALL, ימלועה תופיטעה ןויכרא
Thank you Hashem for allowing me to use my gift of music to inspire and entertain jepwds wfcordld1wi7de@. gSinmcea I icla.ncnootm fully
express my gratitude to you in mere words, please consider my singing on this album as my way of saying "Thank you'!!!

I am extremely thankful to ךרבתי 'ה for giving me the תוכז to use music
as a means to connect to Him. I believe that music has the power
to connect our souls to אוה ךורב שודקה. The moving songs "ילק" and

I was blessed to have the opportunity to work together
with Chayala Neuhaus on this beautiful project. I
would like to express my gratitude and have much
appreciation to all those who have supported me:
My parents, Aryeh and Adina Pearlman; my sisters,
Shoshana, Miriam, and Zahava; my grandparents,
Ruthie Halpert, Harvey and Carol Pearlman, and the
rest of my family and friends who continuously
support and encourage me. Thank you!

C-NOTE
STUDIOS
PRESENTS MIRACLES II
COMPOSED BY C. NEUHAUS | A DONI GROSS PRODUCTION
SUNG BY YEHUDA KIRSCHNER | FEATURINGימ לDועOהV תIוDפי טPעהE AןויRכרLאMAN
pdfcd17@gmail.com
Yehuda- watching your amazing talent come to life on this album has been a true gift!
Doni Gross - for making miracles II a smooth sailing ride, and for your magnificent
arrangements.
Chavy Klein - miracle of miracles, I lucked out in the mentor department. It means
the world to me to have your keen eye to rely on in all aspects of production. Rest
assured that I will continue to seek your sage advice until you are thoroughly fed up
with me. Which will hopefully be never ;)
I would like to extend my thanks to Hakadosh
My esteemed clients and friends, Mrs. Chanie Bandman, Mrs. Chavi Ohayon, Mrs. Baruch Hu for making this “miracle” happen. I was
Batya Krasnow, Mrs. Rena Basya Newmark, and my Camp Bnos family (just to name privileged to meet the talented Chayala Neuhaus, who
a few) - thank you for sweeping me up in the whirlwind of your creative journeys and was the shaliach to produce this wonderful album. The
challenging me musically year after year! words of the songs were so meaningful to me and really
touched my heart. Thank you Chayala and Doni Gross
The amazing team at C-note Studios including but not limited to Miri, Rikki, Chaya Nomi, Rivky and
for all your hard work in developing and bringing out my
everyone else - kudos to you for keeping the music afloat!
talent.
My dear parents, who paid for the lessons, spared no efforts, listened to every single song I wrote
I would also like to thank my parents who always
more than once or twice, and still continue to be my number one guides in life. Your support is
believed in me and encouraged me to follow my dreams,
invaluable.
and to use the talents Hashem has given me. Thank you
My unbeatable band of sisters who have always dreamed big for me and who envisioned this long to my siblings, Yaakov Meir and Frumie, Zalmy and Leah,
before I ever did! I know this album would not be gracing the shelves without your steady stream of and Esther for being my constant support and encour-
shvester-style feedback and encouragement. agement throughout. Enjoy!
My husband and kids, for being my number one cheering squad from start to finish, and for partic-
Yehuda
ipating in my crazy musical adventures time and again. For “holding back the tides” and for all the
other songs you’ve inspired, I just don’t have the words.
I am exceptionally grateful to Hakadosh Boruch Hu for leading me through life’s song one beautiful
day at a time. Miracles II has truly been an unending series of miracles, and I’m thankful for every
single one.
Chayala

BARUCH LEVINE You are someone I have looked upto since I first
met you, over 15 years ago. You’re a role model to what it means to be a
true Ben Torah in the music industry. Thank you for intrusting me with the
opportunity to record your "timeless" classics. I hope that through this album
people will feel the appropriate mindset for Sefira and the 3 Weeks. Hashem should
continue to shower upon you this special gift of inspiring Yidden through song.
YEHUDA BRODY, YEHUDA KIRSCHNER, MOSHE ELIYAHU LAZAR, MEIR RICHTER, ARYEH
RUBIN, YISROEL SALAMON, & MENDY SCHACHTER Thank you for sweetening the album
with your beautiful voices. You each put in hard work and BH it has really paid off.
JOEY NEWCOMB, DOVID PEARLMAN, AVRUMI SCHWEBEL, RIVIE SCHWEBEL,
TZVI SILBERSTEIN, & SHLOMO SIMCHA Thank you for graciously lending your
beautiful voices to this project and truly bringing it to the next level!
SHUMI ZELMANOVITZ This album has definitely showed your
dedication to KITR. Brooklyn misses you. You always keep it fresh and
exciting, and are a real pleasure to work with!! We should continue to
inspire through music.
BORUCH SHAPIRO, YACOV SPRECHER, & TZVI ELI LEFKOWITZ
The authentic DeG Choir. Special thanks to Boruch for going
through all the mixes!
YEHUDA GROSS Thank you for always coming through in the
clutch and for all of your creative input!
YOCHEVED HERZOG Thank you for your high quality
graphic work! It looks beautiful!
AVRUMI JAFFA Once again, you stepped up big time and
delivered. Thank you!

To Our Dear Listeners,
We are living in scary times. The “A Kumzitz In The Rain” series was
created to help set the mood to allow the listener to properly feel
the emotions of the days of Sefira and 3 weeks, which are the
saddest times of the Jewish year. This year is different, as unfortu-
nately all of our lives are turned upside down. We don’t know what
tomorrow will bring. There is fear, uncertainty, and anxiety. This
year it's not so hard to feel for these sad times. However, I feel it’s
very appropriate to have this album based on the Shalsheles
classics, since most of these sweet tunes are based on the words of
Dovid Hamelech. Dovid, as we know, had many hardships in his life.
But even at the darkest of times, even when most were unable to
see any light, Dovid was able to connect himself to Hashem, to seek
light and seek comfort. He showed us ― his children ― that no
matter how dark things may look, we can, and need to hold onto
Hashem, as he is the ONLY source of salvation! May we all be safe
and be zocheh very soon to the ultimate geulah, when we no longer
know of pain, the geulah sheleyma bimheyra viyameynu!
- Doni

«Worship G-d with joy» - a verse from Tehillim that became the founda-
tion of Chassidus.
The song and the nigun are an inseparable part of the Chassidic avoda –
service. The nigun, it is said, uplifts and inspires man .
Rabbi Hillel of Paritch, one of the greatest followers of the Mitteler Rebbe
and the Rebbe Tzemach Tzedek, once said: «One who has a feel for ne-
gina, has a better feeling of Chassidus.»
While visiting at various Chabad Houses in venues at far-flung corners
of the earth, on many varied and important occasions, it became ob-
vious,when singing the Seder Nigunim of our Rebbeim, that there is a
lack of clarity among many regarding the correct order and nigun of each
of the Rebbeim. I wondered how I could convey the emotive feelings of
yearning and longing that the Seder Nigunim brings forth. How could I
make the proper order and background of each nigun of the Seder Nigunim
accessible to all, internalizing and integrating this sublime Seder, which is
also sung in shuls of Anash at the Seudas Moshiach and conclusion of the
Shovuos holiday?
After tremendous spiritual and material effort and investment, I am very
pleased to present to you The Nigunim Project.
This project, unlike its predecessors, was produced and recorded in a very
unique way. Every note, every movement, was recorded once, twice or even
three times, until we reached the finished product, which is absolutely
accurate, to our professional and musical satisfaction, in a meticulous
and painstaking manner, in order to preserve the original nigun as it
ימלועה תופיטעה ןויכרא
pdfcd17@gmail.com
Hanigunim Booklet.indd 2 21/11/2017 16:35
j.music booklet archive
pdfcd17@gmail.com
was transmitted from previous generations to this generation, and to
generations to come.
The Nigunim presented on this unique album are the very nigunim that the
Rebbe ordered to be sung during the designated times, at the Farbrengens
in 770.
As a addition, we also included the arrangement of the Alter Rebbe’s Nigun,
the nigun of Arba Bavos followed by Nye Zhuritze Chloptzi, in accordance
with the order sung by the Rebbe, so that they will be sung properly.
Heartfelt thanks to R’ Sheya Segal of Kehot Publishing House - for
his guidance and attention to every detail throughout the recording and
singing stages of the project, as well as to R’ Shimi Segal, R’ Simcha
Friedman and R’ Mordechai Brodsky.
To Moshe Laufer for his dedicated work, and the outstanding result -
the magnificent arrangements which intensify the vibrancy and joy of the
nigunim. The nigunim, firmly rooted in all that is holy and sublime, coupled
with innovative sound and state of the art technology, while preserving the
fiery authentic tradition, brought the project to fruition .
Many thanks to the talented vocalists: R’ Avraham Fried, R’ Yoni Shlomo,
R’ Simcha Friedman, Rabbi Eli Marcus, R. Berel Zucker and R’ Shmil.
B’Birkas Chassidim,
Moshe Dov

דיגמה ברה - בוט-םש-לעבה ןוגינ The Baal Shem Tov, the Maggid
ןקזה ר”ומדאו שטירזעממ of Mezritch and the Alter Rebbe
סוקרמ ילא ׳ר :עוציב Performed by: R’ Eli Marcus
ןושארה קלחה :םיקלח השולש הז ןוגינל The nigun has three parts: the first
דיגמל ינשה ,בוט-םש-לעבל סחוימ part is attributed to the Baal Shem Tov,
.ןקזה ר”ומדאל ישילשהו ,שטירזממ the second to the Maggid of Mezritch,
לכב ךא ,דואמ םימוד םיקלחה תשולש and the third is attributed to the Alter
.תובהלתהה תרבגתמו לוקה קזחתמ קלח Rebbe.
The three parts are very similar, but in
each part the tone is strengthened and
the enthusiasm increases.

התא יל-א Keili Atoh
ןקזה ר”ומדא ןוגינ The Alter Rebbe’s Nigun
בוד השמ ׳רו לימש ׳ר :עוציב Performed by: R’ Shmil & R’ Moshe Dov
ר”ומדא לש םינוגינה תרשעמ דחא אוה הז ןוגינ This nigun is one of the ten nigunim composed by the
יקלא ,ךדואו התא יל-א“ :ןה ויתולימו ,ןקזה Alter Rebbe. The words are: «You are my G-d and I will give
.)ח”כ ,ח”יק םילהת( .”ךממורא thanks to You; You are my G-d and I will exalt You.”
תעב הז ןוגינ ןגנל וגהנ וניאישנ וניתובר תיבב This nigun was traditionally sung at the homes of our Re-
גח לילב רדסה םויסבו ,’ונלש םימ‘ תביאש bbeim, during the drawing of “Mayim Shelanu” and at the
וסוכמ ןייה תא הרזח גזמ יברהש תעב ,חספה conclusion of the seder on the night of Pesach, as the Rebbe
.קובקבל – והילא לש poured back the wine from Kos shel Eliyohu into the bottle.
םיוותל לעמ עיפומ ,)א ךרכ( םינוגינה רפסב In Sefer Hanigunim (Vol. 1), the title «Nigun Kos shel Eliyohu
-םש רותב ”איבנה והילא לש סוכ ןוגינ“ :יוניכה HaNovi» appears above the musical notes as a sub-title.
צ”יירה ר”ומדא םשב רמא יברה .ןוגינל הנשמ The Rebbe said in the name of the Rebbe Rayatz that this
ןמויב .”והילא יוליגל הלוגס“ אוה הז ןוגינ יכ melody is «a segulah for the revelation of Eliyahu.» In the
:)שידיאב( הז ןוגינ הנוכמ ,צ”יירה ר”ומדא לש Rebbe Rayatz’s diary, this melody (in Yiddish) is called
.והילא יוליג לש העונתה ,”העונת והילא-יוליג“ «Gilui Eliyohu Movement,» the movement of the revelation
יוליג‘ ףקתשמו רייטצמ הזה ןוגינבש רמול הצור of Eliyohu, meaning to say that the revelation of Eliyohu
.הניגנהו םילילצה רושימב ’והילא resonates and is reflected in the tone and music.
Choir arrangements: Simcha Friedman & Yedidim

The
Kapelye
j.music booklet archive

Originally, in Yiddish, Der Mitteler Rebbe’s Kapelye), a nigun
composed by the Mittler Rebbe’s choir. The nigun was endeared by
the Rebbe.
The Kapelye was comprised of two divisions: vocalists, and musi-
cians. When they played on weekdays, it was similar to the service
of the Leviim in the Bais Hamikdosh, who sang during the sacrificing
of the public and obligatory sacrifices that were offered at the Bais
HaMikdosh.
The main function of the choir was to sing and play in the Mitteler
Rebbe’s presence, when he appeared in public- while lighting
Hanukkah candles, going out in the field on Lag BaOmer and more.
The Mitteler Rebbe was very fond of the choir and was involved in
managing its affairs through various instructions regarding their
singing. Several times, the Rebbe carried out spiritual acts through
the Kapelye. .
Other melodies composed by members of the choir are the nigun for
Birkas Kohanim and for the .blessing of the Kohanim and the nigun
Nye Zuritze Chluptzi .

The nigun was composed by the Chossid Rabbi Aharon Charitonov of
the city of Nikolaev. This melody was considered to be the nigun of
the Rebbe Rayatz, who was particularly fond of it, and even gave the
nigun its name, «The Beinoni,» saying that this nigun corresponds to,
and reflects the struggle of the Beinoni, as is explained in the Tanya.
The connection of the nigun to the Redemption :
On the night of Simchas Torah, 5749, the Rebbe related to the Seder
Nigunim of all the Rebbeim, and spoke of the nigun of the Rebbe
Rayatz, known as the “Beinoni”, in connection with preparation
for the Geulah:
«It is necessary to comment on the nigun of the Rebbe, my father-
in-law, which he called the» Beinoni- connected with the coming of
Moshiach Tzidkenu, because» our actions and work « that bring the
Geulah (beginning with the psak din (ruling) of the Rambam that ‘one
mitzvah can bring favorable judgment for him and the entire world,
and effected for him and them, deliverance and salvation) «Most of
the Torah is attributed to) ( as the Torah speaks to the majority) the
avodah (service) of the Beinoni which is» from the character of every
person, after which every person will draw, «and therefore the work
of the righteous who are few.

A spirited nigun sung by Chazzan Rabbi Yechiel Halperin in Lubavitch in the presence of
the Rebbe Rashab and the Rebbe Rayatz.
The words of the melody, expressing hope and confidence that the redemption is near, are
part of the Shabbos and Yom Tov Mussaf prayers.
The Rebbe taught this nigun on Simchas Torah 5724, before daybreak.
Before the Rebbe began to teach the nigun, he asked for a Siddur, and began to teach the
nigun with the Siddur open before him. He sang the end of the nigun without words.
After he finished singing once, he said, «This melody was sung in Rostov and Lubavitch
before the Rebbe, and seemingly even on Rosh Hashana during the “Kesser” prayer,
before the Rebbe.
When they finished singing, the Rebbe turned to R’ Reuven Dunin, who was present, and
bid him sing the nigun again. R’ Reuven had not yet mastered the nigun, and the Rebbe
said: «In this nigun the central focus is the text- words that express our confidence in
redemption, and not the tune!»
The next day, during the Mussaf prayer, the new melody was sung in «Kesser», which the
Rebbe began in a very high tone, almost shouting, «Hu Elokeinu».
Hu Elokeinu was the last nigun taught by the Rebbe.

The words of the nigun are from the text of the Yom Tov prayer.
This nigun was taught by the Rebbe in the pre-dawn hours of SimchasTorah .
The nigun is comprised of two parts.
In the first part, all the words of the nigun are sung in sequence, expressing the life of
the righteous.
The second part, in which words and passages are repeated, expresses the experiences
of the Baal Teshuvah.
When the Rebbe taught this nigun, he said, “In this nigun there are two melody stanzas
and two , melodies. One – corresponding to the righteous who follow the straight path,
and the second corresponding to the baal teshuvah, with the second stanza repeated
twice, like the movement of a ba’al teshuva who acts stubbornly.
The Rebbe concluded that this is also the difference between the month of Nissan
- symbolizing the service of the righteous, and the month of Tishrei -symbolizing
the work of the Ba’alei Teshuvah. The Rebbe taught two melodies to the words «Ata
Vechartanu,» and so he sang again and again and again until the menagnim mastered
the nigun. Then the Rebbe ordered them to sing and encouraged them for many
minutes. The next day, after singing the new nigun, the Rebbe said: «In the nigun of
the Baal Teshuvah, every movement is repeated twice, because their work is ‘kiflayim
l’tushiya’- twice as great ».

Torah hakafos of the Alter Rebbe

ON THE ROAD TO PERFECTION
As we all know, there is nothing in this world that is totally flawless.
Nonetheless, we continually strive to come as close as we can to that un-
attainable goal known as perfection.
When Aderet Music undertook the Od Yosef Chai project, our goal was to
utilize today’s ever improving technology. We took 100 year old record-
ings of renowned Chazzan Yossele Rosenblatt and made them come alive,
by improving sound quality and removing all extraneous noise and static,
while preserving the magic and beauty that was Reb Yossele Rosenblatt.
Od Yosef Chai Volume 1 was released in 2008, 75 years after Reb Yossele’s
passing, and we were overwhelmed by the emotional outpouring of grati-
tude from Rosenblatt devotees worldwide. 100 years after Yossele sang
many of these songs, his growing legions of fans were finally able to hear
firsthand the true beauty of his legendary voice and the tremendous feel-
ing and heart with which he imbued every word and every note.
Od Yosef Chai Volume 2, the next volume in this monumental series, was
released one year later, featuring 72 breathtaking minutes of Yossele’s
golden voice enhanced by new instrumentation and choral pieces. Od
Yosef Chai Volume 3, released less than one year ago featuring even
greater advances in the science of restoration, was such a musical master-
piece that it caught the attention of major media outlets including the
New York Times, Canadian Broadcasting Company, National Public Radio
and more.
As technology continues to advance, we are moving closer and closer to
our goal of musical perfection with the release of Od Yosef Chai Volume
4. With background noise virtually eliminated, the clarity of this album is
so pristine that we came to call it “Up Close and Personal” and it is as if
Yossele is right there in the room, singing these songs especially for you.
It is as close to perfection as we can get at this time.

of dust, worms and maggots; 'and before Whom you will give justification and reckon-
ing?' — before the King Who reigns over kings, the Holy One, Blessed is He.

again; and what I have sinned before You may You obliterate with Your abundant mercy,
but not through suffering or serious illness.

Amar Rabi Elozor amar rabi chanina, talmedai chachomim marbim shalom b'olom shene'e-
mar v'chol bonai'ich lemudei hashem v'rav shalom bonai'ich, al tikrei bonai'ich ela boy'nai'ich.
Shalom rov l'ohavei sorosecha v'ain lomo michshol. Yehi shalom b’cheileich, shalvah b’armi-
nosayich L’maan Achai V’Rayai adabra na shalom boch L’maan bais Hashem Elokeinu avaksha
tov loch Hashem oiz L’amo yitein, Hashem Yivareich es amo ba’shalom
TRANSLATION
Said Rabbi Elozor in the name of Rabbi Chanina: The disciples of the sages increase peace
throughout the world, as it is said: And all thy children shall be taught of the L-rd; and great shall
be the peace of thy children. Read not here banayich, thy children, but bonayich, thy builders.—
Great peace have they that love Thy Torah; and there is no stumbling for them. Peace be within
thy ram part, tranquility within thy palaces. For my brethren's and companion's sake, I would fain
speak peace concerning thee. For the sake of the house of the L-rd our G-d I would seek thy
good. The L-rd will give strength unto His people; the L-rd will bless His people with peace. —a
quotation from the end of the first tractate of the Babylonian Talmud, included in the order of
services of the eve of theSabbath as well as in the additional service of Sabbath and holiday
mornings. In the traditional chant used in the study of the Talmud a colorful pattern of robusto
tones, contrast and coloratura in falsetto has been woven by the composer and has been exe-
cuted by him in his own inimitable manner.

:ןֵמָ א :םִיָלָׁשּ ורְי ויָמֲ חַרְב הֵנֹוּב .םשה הָּתַא ְךּורָּב .ּוניֵמָ יְב הָרֵהְמִּ ב ׁשֶ דּקַה ריִע םִיַלָׁשּ ורְי הֵנְבּו
TRANSLITERATION
Rachem na Hashem Elokeinu al yisroel amecha, v'al yerushalayim erecha, v'al tzion
mishkan k'vodecha, v'al malchus beis dovid meshichecha, v'al habayis hagadol v'hako-
dosh shenikra shimcha olov. Elokeinu, ovenu, reanu, zunanu, parnisanu, v'chalkilanu,
v'harvichanu, v'harvach lonu Hashem elokeinu meharo m'kol tzorosanu. V'na al
tatzrichanu Hashem elokeinu lo lidei matnas busar v'dom, v'lo lidei halvo'osom
Ki im liyodicha hamilaya, hapisucha, hakidosha, v'harchova, shelo neivosh v'lo nikoleim
l'olam voed. U'vnei yerushalayim eir hakodesh bemhara b'yomeinu. Boruch ata Hashem
bonei b'rachamov yerushalayim, amen.
TRANSLATION
Have mercy, we pray Thee, 0 L-rd our G-d, upon Israel Thy people, upon Jerusalem Thy
city, upon Zion the abiding place of Thy glory, upon the kingdom of the house of David
Thine anointed, and upon the great and holy house that was called by Thy name. 0 our
G-d, our Father, feed us, nourish us, sustain, support and relieve us, and speedily 0 L-rd
our G-d, grant us relief from all our troubles. We beseech Thee,O L-rd our G-d, let us
not be in need either of the gifts of flesh and blood or of their loans, but only of Thy
hand, which is full, open, overflowing and ample, so that we may not be ashamed nor
confounded for ever and ever. Rebuild Thou also Jersualem the holy city speedily in our
days. Blessed art Thou, 0 L-rd, who in Thy compassion rebuildest Jersualem. Amen.
—the third paragraph of the grace after meals. This impassioned plea on behalf of Israel
and Jerusalem, the house of David and the Temple, is heightened by repetition
in a crescendo variant. A more subdued petition for personal sustenance follows. Then,
as the petitioner asks for freedom from dependence on humיaמnל hועeהlp ,ת pוrפeיfטeעrrהi nןgוי tכoר אbe
beholden to G-d alone, the tone rises again. The recjtative ends on a note of confidence

in the rebuilding of Jerusalem.

Yish-ta-bach shimcho lo-ad malkeinu, hokeil hamelech hagodol ve-ha-kodosh
bashomayim uvo-oretz ki lecho noeh Hashem elokeinu velokei avoi-seinu shir ush-vocho
hallel vezimra oiz umem-sholo netzach gedulo ugevuro kedusho umalchus. Berochois
ve-hoi-do-ois mei-ato ve-ad oilom boruch ato Hashem keil melech godol ume-hulol ba-
tish-bo-chois keil ha-hoido-ois adon ha-nif-lo-ois haboi-cheir be-shirei zimra melech keil
chai ho-oi-lo-mim
TRANSLATION
May your name by praised forever – our King, the G-d, the great and holy King – in
heaven and on earth. Because for You is fitting – O Hashem, our G-d and the G-d of our
forefathers – song and praise, lauding and hymns, power and dominion, triumph, great-
ness and strength, praise and splendor, holiness and sovereignty, blessings and thanks-
givings from this time and forever. Blessed are You, Hashem, G-d, King exalted through
praises, G-d of thanksgivings, Master of wonders, Who chooses musical songs of praise
ימלועה תופיטעה ןויכרא
– King, G-d, Life-giver of the world.

Zaro chayo vika-yomo zaro di loo yifsook vedi loo yivtoil, mipis-gomei oi-ray-so,lechol
kehulu kadishu hudein, ravrivayo, im zei-rayo, taflo une-shayo. Malko deolmo yivureich
yaschoin, yapish chayei-choin, veyasgei yomeichoin, veyitein acko lish-nei-choin, vesis-
porkun vesish-teizvun min kol uku umin kol marin bee-shin. Moron di vishmayo yehei
bi-saade-choin, kol zeman ve-idon venomar omein
TRANSLATION
Living and surviving offspring, offspring who will neither interrupt nor cease from the
words of the Torah – to this entire holy congregation, adults along with children, infants
and women. May the King of the universe bless you, make your lives fruitful, increase
your days, and grant length to your years. May He save you and rescue you from every
distress and from all serious ailments. May the Master in heaven come to your assistance
at every season and time. Now let us respond: Amen.

ma lecho hayom ki sonus hayarden tisov le-ochor hehorim tirkedu ke-eilim giv-oois

TRANSLATION
What ails you sea, that you flee, O jordan, that you turn backward?,
O mountains that you skip like rams, O hills, like young lambs,
Before the L-rds presence, did I, the earth tremble,
Before the presence of the G-d of Jacob, Who turns the rock into a pond of water,
The flint into a flowing fountain.

טלעוו רעד ףיוא תוגאד עלא ןופ
,ביילג ךיא תמא ןא טימ ןימאמ ינא
,ןאלפ ןראלק א טימ תמא ןוא רשוי טימ
.ןייז רשקמ ךימ ןטאט םוצ םעד ךרוד
,השועו השע ודבל אוהו ,האנה עדעי ןוא גאטייוו עדעי
,עסיורג רענעלפ ןכאמ וצ דאש א ,העפשה יד למיה ןופ טמוק
,תולעפתה טשינ פאכ ךאז םוש ןייק ןופ ,ריר ןוא ריר ןדעי ייב זיא תיטרפ החגשה יד
,סעלא ריד ראפ ךאד טוט רע רימ ראפ ןהוט וצ טוג תילכת א טימ סעלא
,זמרמ רימ זיא'מ למיה ןופ המכח טימ
,תולעפתה טשינ פאכ ךאז םוש ןייק ןופ
,ן'יבר םוצ ןעמוקעג זיא דיא א ,ליוו עמאמ עדעי ,ךיז טסולג עטאט עדעי
,ןבעג הכרב א םהיא לאז ,ליטש ןוא ליואוו ,לדייא ,ןעגנולעג רעדניק
,םיניינע עפיט יד ןגאז ךייא לעוו ךיא דיא 'ר .סיורג ןיוש ןענעז ייז ןעוו ךיוא תחנ ןהעז
.םינפ ןייד פא ךיד שאוו ,גאט ןוא גאט ןדעי
,הוט ךיא טקנופ סאד ,הירפוצ ןדעי ,יבר רעבא ,גערפ ךיא טוג ךיא הוט וצ
,וצרעד רעסאוו ליפ טימ ,ךיא שאוו םינפ ןיימ ,געט ןוא טכענ ייז ראפ טיירג ךיא ןיב סעלא
,ןרעלקרע הנווכ יד ,ךייא לעוו ךיא דיא 'ר ייא ,גונעג ךנחמ טשינ ךיא ןיב רשפא
טימ ראנ ,רעסאוו ןייק טימ טשינ שאוו .גולק וצ ראג רעדא ,גיד'תומימת ךיא ןיב וצ

One summer morning, I was on the way to work
(camp). I was thinking about how I wasn’t
feeling good about where I was holding spiritually,
but was rather feeling pretty low. I parked the
car, took my ukulele out of the back seat and
wrote this song on the spot. The connection of the
low part and high part is that the tzadik is the
one who knows that the way to greatness is by
I was sitting at a Kiddush on Shabbos morning never giving up. You may fall 7 times, but through
with a Talmid of Yeshiva Ateres Shimon and he the falling, you have the opportunity to pick yourself
said to me “Rabbi! I think you have to be Breslev up and reach even higher heights. You just need
to be b'simcha.” Right then I started singing this to realize that it’s never too late.
song. The rest of the words came naturally.
I was on the way to Lakewood for Shabbos
for a simcha that I was going to perform/sing at
when the GPS kept adding time to the journey
and getting delayed. At one point I felt like
I wasn’t going to make it for Shabbos and it
I was sitting at farbrengen on the 18th of Elul bothered me to the point that I started wondering
with some holy Chevra. This song came to me in why Hashem would do this at the baal simcha’s

I was davening mincha in a Shul
in Queens called Kesser Torah when
I noticed that standing in front of me
was every different type of Jew. It hit
me that we’re all davening to the same
Melech because we’re all bnei hamelech.
I immediately wrote the tune and words
and recorded it on my phone.
I was shopping one Erev Shabbos
in Gourmet Glatt when this niggun
popped into my head, and I quickly
This song was written in a very serious moment. I
was going through a certain tough situation in my
life and one day I was reading Rabbi Sandler’s
book on music and how it could uplift broken hearts. I was sitting one Motzei Shabbos eating
On the same page, he quotes the Gemara that Melaveh Malka when I just started humming
says “shlosha meishivin daato shel Adam, v’eilu hein:
kol, maareh, v’rei’ach.” This means that 3 things can
relax your mind, one of them being “Kol.” Rashi
explains that this is music. That became the low
part. The words of the high part were given to me
a few lines later when the Vilna Gaon was quoted
to have said that even when you’re in the deepest
darkness because of different pain you experience, you
can revitalize your heart with song.
this tune. I loved it, so I quickly recorded it.
The words were added later when I heard the
story of a kiruv Rabbi who was asked a strange
question by a non-observant Jew. The Jew asked
the Rabbi why religious Jews seem to have a
twitch before they eat anything. The rabbi then
realized that unfortunately a Bracha had became
just a movement of the mouth to many of us.
I was sitting in Yeshiva Ateres Shimon
teaching first Seder when I saw the following
vort in the Sefer Noam Elimelech. The word
mei’ayin which is usually translated as “from
where?” actually has a deeper meaning. It means
Written by the Blumstein brothers in honor of the “from Ayin:” from a place in you life called ayin-
birth of Aryeh’s 4th child at the vacht nacht. meaning “nothing.” When you come to that place
When I heard the song, I asked them if I where you realize that you're nothing and Hashem
could have it. It struck a chord in my heart. is everything then yavo ezri- help will come! As
soon as we learned the vort, I wrote the song.
Each song was written from an
experience. I never sat down to
write a song. They all came to me in
entertainment of this album and try
to hear the message of the songs. It
will make the experience much greater.
I hope this album brings smiles and
inspiration. If it effects one person in
a positive way then it was all worth

I was davening mincha in a Shul
in Queens called Kesser Torah when
I noticed that standing in front of me
was every different type of Jew. It hit
me that we’re all davening to the same
Melech because we’re all bnei hamelech.
I immediately wrote the tune and words
and recorded it on my phone.

MESSAGE: Each song was written from an
experience. I never sat down to
write a song. They all came to me in
moments.
Please enjoy the deepness and
entertainment of this album and try
to hear the message of the songs. It
will make the experience much greater.
I hope this album brings smiles and
inspiration. If it effects one person in
a positive way then it was all worth
it.

W
ith gratitude to Hashem Yisborech we hereby present a collection of
songs from the melodic treasury composed by our father, Reb Yecheskel
Honig ל"ז.
Reb Yecheskel ל"ז fled with his parents to London England shortly before the
outbreak of World War II. He learned in the Staines Yeshiva, founded and led
by the Lieger Rov, Reb Chaim Yitzchok Wiengarten ל"צז. He also learned several
years with Hg”r Shloime Baumgarten ל"צז and Hg”r Yakov Tietelbaum ל"צז. He
frequently travelled to Antwerp to his Rebbe and mentor Reb Itzikel of Pshevorsk
ל"קוצז.
He served as a regular Shliach Tzibbur during the Yomim Noroim in the Beis
Hamedrash of his Rebbe the Leiger Rov for twenty years, where his warm teffilos
roused the hearts of the mispallelim. He used to play the violin and showed a
particular talent in composing nigunim full of feeling and warmth.
Having extended much time and effort, we hereby present the first volume
containing Nigunim of Shabbos and Yom Tov that our father ל"ז composed. It is our
fervent hope that they will be received with pleasure and will be treasured by all
those who knew and revered our father ל"ז. They will quench the thirst of those
who yearn for songs and Zemiros composed without any influence from the
modern music of today, by an ellicher chasidishe yid of yesteryear.
We thank ׳ה the הרמז ירישב רחוב for enabling us to present this
treasure of sacred Nigunim and may we soon be
zoiche to the rebuilding of the Beis Hamikdosh,
הננרבו שדח ריש רישנ םשו ,אלמת ןויצ ריע שדקמה הנבי
ר"יכא ,הלענ
Family Honig

Shabbos.
A time of physical rest.
A time of spiritual elevation.
A time for family bonding and last but not least…
a time for intent prayer and joyous song.
Through the music in this album, I strive to
present the contrast between Shabbos joy and
sincerity. The songs portray the gaeity and
jubilance of Shabbos, while the Teffilos (Prayers)
represent the magical interactions we encounter
on Shabbos, on a deeper and more mystical level.
It is my sincere hope that you enjoy this album,
and take some of these timeless melodies to grace
your Shabbos tables.
Yanky

ךנוצר לא ךדבע ךושמ
were written by the Kabbalist
ליא ומכ ךדבע ץורי
Rabbi Elazar Azikri (1533-
ךרדה לומ הוחתשי
1600). He was a disciple of the
ךתודידי ול ברעי
famed mystic Rabbi Isaac Luria
םעט לכו ףוצ תפונמ
of Safed. This text was first
םלועה ויז האנ רודה
sung by the early Chassidim
ךתבהא תלוח ישפנ
at Seudah Shlishis, which is
הל אנ אפר ,אנ לא אנא
a time of special yearning for
ךוויז םעונ הל תוארהב
Hashem.
אפרתתו קזחתת זא
םלוע תחמש הל התיהו
“Beloved of my soul, merciful
Father, draw Your servant to רע׳תמא רעד ךאד טסיב וד ע“שבר
Your will. “ This version of האופר א קיש ,רימ ןטעב ,םילוח אפור
עטעדנואווראפ עלא ראפ המילש
the song includes a heartfelt
לארשי קלאפ ןייד ןופ תומשנ
entreaty in Yiddish, asking
Hashem, the Healer of the ןלייה ןוא ןקראטש ךיז רימ ןלעוו ןאד
sick, for perfect healing of the קילג עגיבייא רעזנוא ןכיירגרעד ןוא
wounded souls of Israel.

םימלוע רוצ תבש וב יכ
Shabbos Z’miros. The texts
differ in their details, though םידוהיל רוא החמשו החונמ
the themes are all of joyous םידמחמ םוי ןותבש םוי
nature. How honored is the
אימלעו םלע ןובר ה-י

איכלמ ךלמ אכלמ אוה תנא
sanctified is the greatness of
החונמ ןתנ רשא ןוילע לא ךורב
Hashem when we rest and bear

.רמוחב ו חpdורfבc dע1פ7ש@ ןgומmהa לilע.c ןoמmטיור היבצ אתבסלו טור הבהזו םייח ףסוי אתבסו אבסל הדות .יעיבשה םוימ תיעובש הדירפ התואמ .םיאב םה לוחל שדוק ןיב רבעמה יעגרמ הכלמ הוולמ יטויפ
.םינשו םימי ךרואל ,םייחה תריש תא דחי רישל הכזנש ןיינב לע ,הלואג לע רשש קר אלו ,ותחפשמ ינב וביבס ,רודה שובלב ידוהיה בשי תבשה ךלהמב
ונילעה ינא אלו התא אלש הדונ אוב ,ןרע – ילש עונצהו רשכומה ילקיזומה קיפמה - ןיילק ןרעל ,הניכשב לארשי תסנכ תוקבדו דוחיאל ללפתהש קר אל ,שדקמה תיבל ה"בקה תרזח לע ,לאירא
םימובלא הנומש םהל ורצוויי ,םילשוריל ףתושמ פמרטב "תירקמ'" העיסנ התואמש ונתעד לע עובש וינפלשכ רתונ םדאה .הניכש ןיאו ,שדקמ ןיאו ,תבשה הל הכלה הנהו .שממ לאגנ שח אלא
תופתושה לע ךל הדותו ,ךתוא יל ריכהש םשהל הדות .םישנאו םיקולא יחמשמ םיעפומ ןומהו ,"הכלמ הוולמ" יטויפ םיעבונ עוגעג ותואמ .המחנ שפחמ אוהו ,הסנרפ תעיגיו ץרא ךרד לוע לש
ןולא .יתריציבש יחרזמה חוחינלו ךרדל תמא ףתוש ,ולש השמל .תירוד ךתייערל םג הדות .ךרדל .לארשי שפנ תא דדועלו םחנל םיסנמ
ךודישה לע – קינמה ירא .אפילל ךודישה לע – לטנמולב יבא .לאזר ןתנויל ךודישה לע – שימלח
ילש תבשה ימובלא תרדס תא םתוח הז םובלאש ןוויכמ
לע לומח" טויפל ונחלב לארשי תוצופת לכב רכומ קינמה ברה .קינמה אביקע ברה אבאל םיזירכמ ונא הדועסה חתפב .הכלמ הוולמ תדועס לש ןיזיפשואה םה איבנה והילאו ךלמה דוד
וכותב ללוכה "דחואמה ףוסכא ה-י" תא ודובכל ונטלקה
רמאנה םילהתב ח"כק קרפ ךותמ "היירופ ןפגכ ךתשא" ונחל תא יתטלקה הז םובלאב ."ךישעמ םיללפתמ ונא ,תבשה םות םע ."םייקו יח לארשי ךלמ דוד .אחישמ אכלמ דודד אתדועס איה אד"
:הז ןימוי קיתע טויפל םינוש םינחל העברא
,יחצ םדא ,וביר ישי ,לאזר ןרהא .תבש יאצומ לש "ךל ןתיו" תליפת יהלשב .תבש ולוכש םוי - המלשה הלואגה תא ומע איביש ,ךלמה דוד ערזמ חישמה ךלמ לש ואובל
לע – ץראוז יעורו איג :םירקיה םיחאה .ךרדל תורבחה לע – ילאמ ללהו ירחא האבה "הכלמ הוולמ"ל ולוכ שדקומה ,הז םובלא יכ אצי החגשהבו ,יעיבשה םויה איה תבש
אזלעב ,רעיירב יבצ ףסוי ברה :ףוסכא ה-י
יקירש יתמ .החמשה ןיינע לע – סייו השמ ברה .םכתיא רשקה םצע ,ןכא .עבטל רבעמ לא רבוחמ אוה ףאש זמר והז .יתריציב ינימשה םובלאה אוה ,יעיבשה םויה
ןירג הדוהי :ירחאמ לצה ,ץרמנ שיא ,ןמלק הדוהיל .ןחו תונלבס הברהו ץק ןיא תוריסמ לע – .םובלא רחא םובלא ,ךרבתי םשה ילע עיפשמ ותוא דסחל תעלוק הרדגה וז "עבטל רבעמ"
ריב ילא :ךימחר ויהיו .םכילא עיגת ונלש הרישהו הניגנהש ידכ תובר למעה
רבוו )יקניפ( יכדרמ סחנפ :תבשה .הרמיז לוקו הרישב וילא רבחתהל יל ןתנשכ יתיא למגש הבוטה לע ל-אל הדותב אופא חתפא
לבוי ונימילו ,בל לאכימ השארב דמועלו "ריש ירבד" תרבחל השארבו 'תוכלמ' תלהקמ ,לאזר ןתנוי :הז םובלאב ילש םיחרואל תודוהל יל אוה לודג דובכ
– "םלוע תחמש" אלפנה םובלאב רבעב אצי "הכלמ הוולמ" תורימזמ "ינושש שדח" רישה עיגת וזה הניגנמהש םיגאודש םיציפמה תווצ לכו טר בדנ ,גינפרבליז יתריש לא ףרטצהש ימלו חבש רואו ןר םיקותמה םיחאה ,םחנמ דוד םכח ,רלכיב סחנפ דיסחה
לש עוציבב יתבהאתה .רכב בדנו רוצ ןרוא ידיב םידבועמ גרובזניג ברה יריש ולוכש םובלא .םכתיבל הצפח שפנב םתמתרנש הדבועה ,דחי םכתיא רישל תוכזה דבלמ .רצלמש אפיל :םיל רבעמ
ותובידאב הנענ רשא ,טסברה ןנח ,אוהה טקיורפה קיפמל יתינפ גירח דעצב .רישל וביר ישי :ןיפיקעבו ןירשימב םובלאה תקפהב םיעייסמהו םיפתושה לכל .הכאלמה לא תשגל תוחוכ יל הנתנ ,ילש טקיורפל ףרטצהל
.םכינפלש "הכלמ הוולמ" םובלאב סונוב תעוצרכ הזה רישה תא סינכהל יתלאשמל הברה ףסא ,קחש לאהשע ,םיובלא ימולש ,ירפ ףסא ,עלס יבוק ,הנוי לאומש
הרואיל ,סלזיימ הליה ,ןוטוב יקימ ,גיווצרבליז קילומש ,שורה לגי ,ןיילק רישל ףא אלא ,ריאמ הויזו באילא ברה ,יירוהל תודוהל קר אל תוכזה יקלחב הלפנ הז םובלאב
ריאמ קחצי םיסינ ,תוילגרמ השמ ,ןמ יקוש ,ץכרב ינוי ,ןייטשקא תרפאו םייח ,טור תועיסנל רכז ,דומלת בקעי 'ר לש ונחלב "בקעיל םשה רמא" רישה תא א"טילש אבא םע דחי
.הז םובלאב ונגינש םיאלפנה םינגנהו רגרבניו םיחאה ,ךורב .ל"ז ריאמ הרופיצו ןרהא אתבסו אבס תא רקבל ביבא-לת ריעה לא תותבש יאצומב תויתחפשמה
,"בקעי ידבע ארית לא" וירחא םיזרוח תויחאו םיחא ונאו "בקעיל םשה רמא" רמזמו גהונ אבא
רתוי שגרתמ בלה םובלאו םובלא לכ םעש ולא ,םיביבח םינורחא .הרוש רחא הרוש
רמת יתשא :ייח תניגנמל הדות ,ףד ילע ןאכ םכריכזהל רתויו .הבשקההו תוצעה ,דודיעה לע ,ייתוסיגו ייסיג ,ייתויחאו ייחא לכל הדות
ינא ןפלואב תפסונ העש לכב – תידוהי ללהו ןורהא הליש ונידליו .הלעמ יפלכ תידימת הפיחד לע ,ריאמ-בהר ןויס יתסיגו הידידי רוכבה יחאל תדחוימ הדות
.םכתציחמב חור תרוק לש העש דוע ץימחמ ינאש ךכ לע רצימ .עויסהו תבשקה ןזואה לע ,ריאמ לארשי ןושלהו טעה לק ,יחאל
ימלועה תופיטעה ןויכרא .יפוס ןיא ןומאו ןוגרפ לע הדות ףצומ בלה - טור ליגיבאו השמ ,יתייער ירוה

(דועו א 'ט תינעת).יד רמולמ םכיתותפש ולביש דע
:החמשו תובהלתה ןוגינ 9 דמחה ידלי לש דומיל תועש יפלא תואמ וכותב דגאמה "הרות ילמע" ןוגרא
קחצי ברה ןוגראה ןורטפ לש ולעופ תא ריקוהל שקיב ,ןבר תיב לש תוקונית
ק''כ ידוד לש ותשקבל רוצ זועמ טויפה לע הכונחה גח דובכל ורוקמב ןחלוה ןוגינה .הז ןוגינ ןחלוה ונב יאושינ תחמש דובכל .א"טילש ךולאפ יכדרמ
ןחלה תארשה .טויפל חמש ריש ןיחלהל (ב"בב שיא ןוזח בוחרב) גלשאמ ר''ומדאה
תיב ןינבל םיהמכ ונאשכ 'העושיה ץק ברקו ךישדק עורז ףושח' םילימהמ אוה ןמרביל לארשי ביוט דיוד לזיו ךורב :תולהקמ יולה סחנפ דליהו לזיו ךורב :הריש
.העושיה ץק ברק 'רהמ רבכ היהיש וניתשקב לבא שדקמה ןמסטוג םייח :סקימ ,יצ לאכימ– רונט ינפלוא :ןפלוא
,תימלועה םידידי תלהקמ :תולהקמ טוגפלה יקנעי דליה : הריש :ןוביר הק 8
טנב םייח תורימז ינפלוא - ןמסטוג םייח :סקימו ןפלוא אּיָהַמְתִוְ תּ ָרְוּבּגְ דבַוע איָּכַלְ מַ לֶ מֶ אכָּלְ מַ אוּה ּתְנְאַ אּיָמַלְ עָוְ םלַ עָ ןובּרִ הּיָ
(תבש לילל תורימזה ךותמ ) איָּוָחֲהַלְ ךמָדָקֳ רפַשְׁ

To my parents, thank you for always pushing me to practice and develop my potential. Your belief in me
made it all happen. Thank you for keeping me well grounded, I hope to bring you much Nachas.
To Menachem, you have always been my model!
To Mushkie thank you for pushing me to present myself at aJewishstar competition.
To Shmuel, Chana Beila, Shlomo, Rivka, you're the best siblings in the world! I love you all so much!
Yitzy Waldner, you are such a special Neshama, a true source of inspiration and remarkable middos thank
you for your beautiful compositions, your guidance, your patience and mostly for putting up with me .It was
a great privilege to work at your side.You're truly one of a kind!
Ian, you're the best ! You have true vision when it comes to music. Your soul goes into every piece, it was a
great pleasure and privilege working with you.
Myriam Arman for all your patience, guidance, dedication, caring about my voice as if it was your own.I
could never have done it without you! You work Miracles! May Hashem bless you with long life and good
health to continue your holy work.
Moshe Roth, thank you for all your advice and the incredible choirs that brought this album to anew dimen-
sion.
A very special thanks to Boruch Freedman and family who made a dream become a reality, may Hashem
grant you success and good health always! I have no words to thank yoיuמ לeוnעoהu תghופ י!טעה ןויכרא

Hi, Friends! Levi here!
After months of extensive effort and endless work that our very
talented team have invested solely for your listening pleasure, I
am pleased to present to you my debut solo album!
This album consists of eleven carefully selected songs from
prominent composers, chosen with you in mind, accompanied by
the most beautiful & enjoyable music of the highest quality. Come
join me on this journey, I hope you will enjoy!

עיגנ םגו ץורנ ךלנ דיב די גואדל ךירצ אל - םישק תצק םירבד שי
חילצהל לוכי דחא בלב דחא םע יכ חונמ תונתונ אלש םייחב תולאש
גואדל ךירצ אל - םלועב תומחלמ
הנתמ םה םייחה חוטב אל תצק רחמה לע תוגאד
הדות דיגת טושפ זא גואדל ךירצ אל - םישיערמ םינכשה
םלועב עגר לכ לע עגרל רוצעת זא םישיבכב ץחלהו
בוטה לע קר לכתסת ץורל ךישמת רוצעת לא - ךל השק םא םג

find a true friend is a blessing. ...and to find both in

Avrum Mordche you have an authentic deep love
of music, and a brilliant understanding of how to
build and create an album to best suit the singer.

crafting every song to be a piece of art and bring Avrum

production process with such passion and talent, This album
personally overseeing every detail from song
is really a
choice and lyrics, to arrangement styles and sound.
piece of you !

Avrum Mordche, This album is really a piece of
you. You out did yourself.

AGRADECIMIENTOS
Al Sr. Eduardo Elsztain por su desinteresada colaboración para la realización de este Disco. A Mendy Drachman
por ser el impulsor y motor principal para que grabe mi primer CD de inéditos y por los hermosos temas que
compuso y me cedió generosamente. A Guillermo de Medio por el arduo e incansable trabajo realizado en los
arreglos, mezcla y por ser nuestra guía en todo este largo camino. A Sergio Glantz por ser la persona que me
ayudo a profesionalizar mi trabajo y por haber aportado 2 de sus composiciones para este Álbum. A Eli Suli
por el tema Kol Sason que sin duda creemos que será bailará en las bodas cumpliendo su mayor deseo: que su
música alegre las neshamot de Israel. A Mis Padres por el apoyo y empuje constante para que focalice mi trabajo
solo en alabanzas a Hashem agradecidos por la hermosa familia que nos regaló. A mis queridos hermanos Manu,
Migue y Lizzy por el aliento, y la admiración que manifiestan hacia mi trabajo. A mi querida esposa Laura y
mis 5 hijos Shelo, Esty, Eli, David y Diana que son mi sostén e incentivo para continuar con la difícil tarea de ser
Jazan/Cantante. A mis Suegros por estar siempre presentes. A mis amigos que a pesar de la ansiedad esperaron
tanto tiempo para que este proyecto sea una realidad con el mejor nivel musical posible. A Duby Sutton por su
orientacion y correcciones en las melodías y arreglos, sumado a su incondicional colaboración para el desarrollo
de mi carrera artística. A Ale (Pocho) Salem por incentivarme y alentarme a partir del año 2012, a difundir mas
mi música a través de conciertos y Shows. A Bore Olam por darme este hermoso regalo y poder retribuirle con el
Jazanut y canciones de alabanza.

Looking up, standing on the ground
me and my dreams galaxies apart
but I know somewhere deep inside
I’ve got the strength to get that far,
Climbing on a ladder in the sky
though I’m not an angel maybe I can fly
pushing through still another mile
all I gotta do is try
Chorus
I’m shootin’ for the moon tonight
I ride the waves at the speed of light
I’ve set my goals I made my mind
and if I miss I’ll make it to the stars
we’re gonna make it to the top
I’m shootin’ for the moon tonight
and if I miss I’ll make it to the stars

father I know you believe in me
when it’s rough and I’m feeling doubt
you can raise me up above the clouds,
I can feel the power of a dream
giving me wings taking me beyond
all the pain all the broken hopes
I’m gonna keep holding on
Bridge
Never gonna break apart till I reach my destination
never gonna lose the spark in my soul
gonna be a shining light, gonna be an inspiration
never givin’ up the fight towards my goal

I went and saw simplicity of the holiness of the Second Bais
Hamikdash to see your moisture laden deeds in valleys.
The nations have said to me, turn away, turn away from
Hashem, O nation whose faith in him is perfect, turn away,
turn away, and we shall choose nobility from you. But I replied
to them, what can you bestow upon a nation whole in faith to
him commensurate even with the desert camps encircling?

this production that took three years in the making is Gershy Schwarcz – Thank you so much for your amazing
not nearly enough. The amount of work, dedication, and work on this album, especially your ideas on Kol

for detail have brought out the very best in me, not only

made its mark on the entire album. In addition, thank you To My Dear Wife Raizy and my dear children – Thanks for
for your friendship and guidance throughout the making your constant love and support in everything I do. It is
of this project. you who motivates me to “Shoot for the Moon”.

– Yisroel, thank you once again for supporting my ideas your unwavering belief in me.

!ודסח םלועל יכ בוט יכ 'הל ודוה
!יח לקל וננרי ירשבו י"בל
ק"פל ח"עשת םינתיאה חרי
טרפבו ,הנה דע ינלמג רשא דסחהו בוטה לכ לע ךרבתי 'הל תודוהל הלמ ינושלבו יפב ןיא םילימ
המצעו חכ בורב םולשלו החמשלו םייחל ל"חר הנכס בצממ 'ה ינאיצוה רשאכ ,הנורחאה הנשב
.ךרבתי ומש רזעב
,החמש בורב הזה ןמזל ונעיגהו ונמיקו ונייחהש ,רקוח ב"בל לקל הדואו החנמו הדות שיגא ןכבו
תא ררועל קוזיח ירבדב שודגו אלמ ,"םאלפ המשנ" תרדסמ יעיבשה קלח רואל איצוהל יתיכזו
תוכזל עדונ רבכ םתלוגס רשא ,םימדוקה םיקידצ ירופיסב םיבלושמו ,ךרבתי ומש תנומאל וניבבל
.םישודקה םיקידצה תוכזב בטימד ילימ לכב עשוויהל ,לכל
ישאר לע תואלתה לכ הרבעש תע לכב יכ ,"לאכימ ינימימ" ארקנ יחכונה םובלאה םש תא
םייחה ןיב ינאש ה"בו ,לק תניכש ישאר לעו ,ינימימ םידמוע םיקלא יכאלמ רשא תע לכב יתשגרה
.םדא ינבל ויתואלפנ לכ תא רפסל
ירבדב ןה ,תע לכב יל ועייסו ורזע רשא ולא לכל עיבהל ה"ניגנה לכיה רעשב יתאב תדחוימ הדותו
תורעה וריעהו ןינע לכב ינימיל ודמע רשאל ןהו ,קלחה הז םלוע רואל איצוהל ינצמאלו ינקזחל קוזיח
.ןדיעו תע לכב לארשי תובבל חמשל ,םיברה יכזמ ןיב םכקלח יהי ,תובושח
האלתה לכ תא יתרבע רשא תעב יתחפשמלו יל ועייסו ורזעש ולא לכל הבוט ריכמ יננה דחוימבו
.לארשי יקלא םעמ הלופכו המילש םתרוכשמ יהתו םלעפ 'ה םלשי ,ינתאצמ רשא
ונתיב ינב לכלו ונל לעמ ימשמ עפש עיפשהל ףיסוי הכו 'ה ןתי הכ ,הלפתב יפכ שורפא יתאצבו
.םיחצנ חצנל תויטרפבו תויללכב תאופרו תועושי לארשי לכלו
לארשי ומע ילוח אפור לקל קומע ב"למ האדוהו חבשב
רקיה יסיג תמשנ יוליעל םישנאו םיקלא חמשמ רקיה יריבח תמשנ יוליעל
ה"ע ףסוי 'ב ה ה"ע ןרהא השמ ' ר
ןאמדלעפ ה"ע יולה בוד בקעי ר"ב גרובצראווש ה"ע יולה באז לאומש ר"ב
םייחה רורצב הרורצ ותמשנ אהת םייחה רורצב הרורצ ותמשנ אהת

albums.artist_en: Words to the songs of Mordechai Ben David
albums.full_text:
Written by Shmuel
http://frummusic.blogspot.com - ד”סב
רגידרוו יכדרמ - םידיסח ירימז ףסוא
don't you think it is time
that the sun forever shine א
don't you think it is time
dear daddy of mine ?
Little one it is time
םימחרה בא
and I questioned that too
םימורמ ןכוש םימחרה בא
so let us ask Him together
םימחרב דוקפי אוה ,םימוצעה וימחרב
together me and you
םימימתהו םירשיהו םידיסחה
Father dear, do you hear
םשה תשודק לע םשפנ ורסמש שדוקה תוליהק
our worries our fears
םלוע יקידצ ראש םע הבוטל וניקולא םרכזי
will your eyes ever dry
is your cup filled with tears
Daddy dear - רקי אבא
חמשאו ליגא
Daddy dear tell me please
is it true what they say
יבבלב חמשאו ליגא
in the heavens there's a cup
יביר בירת יביואמ יכ יתוארב
that gets fuller each day
איבת לאוג ןויצלו
and I've heard that Hashem
keeps it close nearby
ונרידא רידא
and He fills it with His tears
וננודא 'ה ונרידא רידא each time that we cry
ץראה לכב ךמש רידא המ
Zaidy told me daddy dear
ץראה לכ לע ךלמל 'ה היהו did he tell you the same
דחא ומשו דחא 'ה היהי אוהה םויב that when sorrow
strikes his people
'ה ךדוא
Hashem feels all the pain
tell me why does He cry
יבבל לכב יקולא 'ה ךדוא
far away in the sky
םלועל ךמש הדבכאו
tell me why daddy dear
are there tears in your eyes ?
ילע לודג ךדסח יכ
היתחת לואשמ ישפנ תלצהו Little one, little one
it is true yes it's true
עורז רוא Zaidy told me years ago
and his dad told him too
םליצי םיעשר דימ וידיסח תושפנ רמוש ער ואנש 'ה יבהוא
החמש בל ירשילו קידצל עורז רוא fathers cry for their children and Hashem does the
same
when we hurt so does He yes He feels our pain
Unity - תודחא
tears of pity from His eyes
Go-d like angels in the sky
in this cup sadly flow
In a garden full of glory
till one day when it's full
The galaxy so brilliantly related
all our troubles will go
Ultimately high
we will dance we will fly
On that first page of our story
in the sky like the birds
Until the time
and I cry little one
Our parents were created cause I'm touched by your words
An envious brother Cain
Threw a blow so mad and chilling
One more question
Tragically he never did recover
daddy dear
It's really so insane
tell me please if you will
All our selfishness that's killing
just how deep is this cup
That stranger who's our
tell me when will it fill
Sister and our brother
1 –
רגידרוו יכדרמ םידיסח ירימז ףסוא
http://frummusic.blogspot.com
Written by Shmuel
http://frummusic.blogspot.com - ד”סב
This תולג it surrounds us
A battlefield who knows Listen brother
We slowly inch ahead Listen friend
We believe it's really close. Just a little smile
A helping hand
Wait! The crack of dawn And we will find
Bright horizon - soon the sunshine A loving kind humanity
Yes, Moshiach's on his way We must teach our children too
All the grief the sorrow slipped away Treat your fellow friend
Like they were you
The past is but a dream Then we all will find
In םילשורי - The שדקמה תיב Some peace of mind
Yes the תולג slipped away In unity
Like the night so swiftly turned to day
Age rushing by
Celebration! Exaltation! Writing chapters full of sorrow
Young and old dancing everywhere Webs of self destruction
we believed it all We are weaving
Heavens standing tall Cause if we don’t even try
Eternal paradise euphoria There's no hope for our tomorrow
the past is but a dream... Say, what's it all worth
If we are not achieving
Pain is so long forgotten
Night forever gone But one thing makes me smile
now here it is Now at last a happy ending
The Crack of Dawn A global union anxiously excited
And in just a little while
ודבלמ דוע ןיא We will join the angels singing
In harmony
םידומו םיוחתשמו םיערוכ ונחנאו
Eternally united
ךורב שודקה םיכלמה יכלמ ךלמ ינפל
ותלוז ספא ודבלמ דוע ןיא
רחשה תלייא –
שאיתת לא Trembling in the darkness
a battlefield who knows
שקבת 'המ שאיתת לא
the soldier crawls ahead
ומצע ענמי לא םדא לש וראוצ לע תחנומ הדח ברח וליפא יכ
for behind him someone goes Oh Heaven
םימחרה ןמ
יינונחת לבקו יילע םחר םימחר אלמ 'ה
Pounding is his heartbeat
ינרסית ךתמחב לאו ינחיכות ךפאב לא
He whispers words of prayer
יימצע ולהבנ יכ 'ה ינאפר ינא ללמוא יכ 'ה יננח
it seems that all his life
יתמ דע 'ה התאו דואמ הלהבנ ישפנו
in a flash may disappear
איבנה והילא
Wait! The crack of dawn
איבנה והילא as it grés the weary trooper
יבשתה והילא now his comrades he can see
ידעלגה והילא Reunited all for victory
דוד ןב חישמ םע ונילא אובי הרהמב
there beneath the sun
ל-אה םשל אניק רשא שיא
in a moment he has conquered
לאיתוקי די לע םולש רשוב שיא
all his fear just slipped away
לארשי ינב לע רפכיו שג שיא
like the night so swiftly turned to day
ויניע ואר רשע םינש תורוד שיא
Trembling in the darkness
וינמיסב רעש לעב ארקנה שיא
Praying for the light
וינתמב רוזא רוע רוזאו שיא
It seems T'may never come
At the darkest of the night Oh yes
םינמח ידבוע לע ףעז שיא
2 –
רגידרוו יכדרמ םידיסח ירימז ףסוא
http://frummusic.blogspot.com
Written by Shmuel
http://frummusic.blogspot.com - ד”סב
Together in the prayer He hears us say םינועמ ימשג תויהמ עבשנו שח שיא
Then our many words become one page together םינש שולש רצע רטמו לט שיא
And forever we are brothers, sisters and friends תחנ ושפנל אוצמל אצי שיא
The children of Hashem unto the very end תחשל תמ אלו םיברועה והולכלכ שיא
As the colours of the rainbow blend together תחפצו דכ וכרבתנ ונעמל שיא
Together as our fathers told יל ינא ןיא םא
We'll trust whatever the future holds יל ינא ןיא םא
We'll gather in our land of gold יל ימ יל ינא ןיא םא
Let heaven's will behold ינא המ ימצעל ינאשכו
?יתמיא - וישכע אל םאו
No longer to be far apart
Every one of us beat with a single heart תמא
Together with Moshiach ןיא ךידעלבמו ןורחא אוה התא תמאו ןושאר אוה התא תמא
We'll be one עישומו לאוג ךלמ ונל
So many sparks become one flame together... ךלמ אנא
םיכלמה יכלמ ךלמ אנא
ןימאמ ינא תרשה יכאלמ ךיכאלמל הוצ
לכ עדוי ומש ךרבתי ארובהש המילש הנומאב ןימאמ ינא ןוילע יתרשמ
םביל דחי רצויה רמאנש םתובשחמ לכו םדא ינב השעמ ינוכרביו םימחרב ינודקפיש
םהישעמ לכ לא ןיבמה יתיבל םאובב
יתטימ יתעצהו יתורנ יתקלדה יכ
ןימאמ ינא תבשה דובכל ייתולמש יתפלחהו
טראוועג ןוא טראוועג באה ךיא .א
,לקניוו א ןיא שרדמ תיב ןיא We are one - דחא ונחנא
דומע ןפיוא לטכעל סאד The holy voice at ינס רה
ץנאט ןטצעל םעד ץנאטעג טאה Revealed to us the laws of life
And through the darkness came His light
ןשאלראפ ךיז סאד טאה טאה To guide and guard us on
לקנוט ןראוועג זיא סע
טרעקאלפוצ ךיז סאד טאה טאה And though we seemed to stand apart
ץנאלג עיינ א ראג טימ Every one of us beat with a single heart
Together we remember
טראוועג ןיא טראוועג באה ךיא We are one
ןעמוק טסעוו יד זא טניימעג
ןעז ריד טלאוועג יוזא באה ךיא The שדקמה תיב in glory then
ןעניפעג טלאוועג יוזא
The heartbeat of Jerusalem
We pray to see it rise again
טראוועג ןוא טראוועג באה ךיא
The home of David's song
טכאנ עצנאג א יוזא
טראוועג ןוא טראוועג ןוא
We know the day will surely come
...טגאוורעד טשינ טשינראג ןוא
The return of our King to Jerusalem
Eternally together
טכוזעג ןוא טכוזעג באה ךיא .ב
We are one
,ןגער ןיא ןוא םעראטש ןיא
,טכאנראפ םענעדלאג ןיא
Yes together locked in harmony
...גאטראפ םענרעבליז ןיא
To walk along the path of unity
As the shining stars will always betogether
טגערפעג ןוא טגערפעג באה ךיא
.ןגעוורעד לאז ךיא ריד יוו
And together we will always stay
טכאלוצ ךיז ןבאה ייז ...הא
For even if we're far apart we pray
....טגאזעג רימ טשינראג ןוא
And so many sparks become one flame together
טכוזעג ןוא טכוזעג באה ךיא
We're together in our hopes each day
3 –
רגידרוו יכדרמ םידיסח ירימז ףסוא
http://frummusic.blogspot.com
Written by Shmuel
http://frummusic.blogspot.com - ד”סב
There will be no more lonely people ,ןענור ןגעלפ ןרערט ןוא
Say it again ...ןעז ריז טלאוועג יוזא באה ךיא
You only have to so the word ...ןעניפעג טלאוועג יוזא
And G-d is there to be your friend
טכוזעג ןוא טכוזעג באה ךיא
When the world’s going wrong טכאנ עצנאג א יוזא
There’s no where to belong ,טראוועג ןוא טכוזעג ןוא
I feel sad when I see lonely people .טגאוורעד טשינ טשינראג ןוא
There’s no hope up ahead
There’s no roof there’s no bed !?ןעזעג ריד ךיא באה ןעוו .ג
It’s so sad when you see lonely people !?ןענופעג ריד ךיא באה ןעוו
Now all we have to do is pray ,טכוזעג טשינ ןיוש באה ךיא ןעוו
Remember each and every day ...רעמ ןיוש טראוועג טשינ ןוא
The reason why we are alive
He’s always there a dearest friend ןגיוא ןיימ ןופ ןרערט ןעוו
He’ll be there till the very end .ןענורעג טשינ ןיוש ןבאה
Through Him we can learn to survive ןייטש א יוו ןראוועג ץראה ןיימ זיא טפאשקנעב ןופ
So don’t you cry
You’ll find the answer there לעטשעג ןייד ךיא באה ןעזרעד
So don’t be shy …םולח א ןיא יוו יוזא
Give it a try דלאוו ןיא טפיול סע שרעה א יוו
Stand up and say a prayer …םיליאה רפועכ
ןקתל רשפא רימ וטספיולטנא יוזא
דחא רקובב בוחרב ךלה קידצה קירוצ םוא ךיז טסרעק ןוא
הארנ אוהו הניפב ול בשוי הלשיומ תא םש האור אוה םואתפ . רועש א ןא טפאשביל טימ
רובש ךכ לכ בוצע ךכ לכ ול …קילב ןכילרעטופ ןייד ןופ
?בוצע ךכ לכ התא המל !הלשיומ הלשיומ :קידצה ול רמוא
?םויה ךל הרק המ תבש ינא
...לאשת לא ,לאשת לא !וניבר יוא !וניבר יוא
םירקי תד תרטע תבש ינא
...תוגאד הברה כ''כ ...תורצ הברה כ''כ ...תויעב הברה כ''כ
םירבדה תרשע תיעיבר תבש ינא
...תונויסינ ינימ לכ ...תואירב ...הסנרפ ...םידליה ...השאה
םירודו תורוד לכל םלוע תירב תוא ינא וינב ןיבו 'ה ןיבו
םוחלל חכ יל ןיא ...םוחלל חכ יל ןיא
םיקלא הלכ וישעמ לכ יבו
!?בוצע היהא אל ךיא .בושו בוש תוליפנ ינימ לכ
םירפסה תישארב בותכ ןכו
...הלשיומ שאייתת לא !הלשיומ ,הלשיומ :קידצה ול רמוא
שאייתת לא
Lonely people - םידדוב םישנא
הברה תלפנ םא וליפא יכ
בוטה תא שפחלו ,תובוטה תודוקנב ,הנומאב קזחתהל ךירצ As I walk in the heat
שאייתת לא ...הלשיומ שאייתת לאו Of the big city streets
! ןקתל רשפאש ...יאדווב לקלקל רשפאש ןימאמ התא םא יכ I get sad when I see lonely people
...תונופטש ,ימנוצ ,תומחלמ ,תודרט אלמ ,תוגאד אלמ םלוע And although the sun shines
ףוסה היהי המ םיגאוד םלוכ ...הניפ לכב רורט They prefer to be blind
...םוי דלוי המ עדוי אל דחא ךא it’s so sad when you see lonely people
...תונוסא הברה כ''כ ,תונויסנ הברה כ''כ ...עדוי אל דחא ףא The answer to a lonely prayer
תושדחב עגר לכ ,םוי לכ Anytime, anywhere
ךושח כ''כ ,רוחש כ''כ הארנ לכה
Can change the direction of life
,םייח ילב ,חור בצמ ילב תובוחרב םיבבותסמ םיבוצע םישנא
...ירמגל םישאוימ
Together we can face the fight
Help each other get it right
לכהש םיעדוי ונחנאו ,םינימאמ ינב םינימאמ ונחנא לבא
הבוטל
Believe and you’ll always get by
...השק כ''כ ...השק כ''כ הארנ לכהש ףא לא So don’t you cry
כ''כ ונתוא בהוא אוה יכ בוט קר השוע ונלש אבאה יכ You’ll find the answer there
ןקתל דימת רשפא ,לפנ םדא ןבש פ''עאו So don’t be shy
רואה תא אוצמלו ...וכות ךותבש בוטה תא שפחל קר Give it a try
בצמ לכבו דחא לכב רוא הברה שי יכ Stand up and say a prayer
There will be no more lonely people
He’ll do the best thing for you

Dear friends,
BH This has been such an awesome year, culminating with the arrival of my debut album!
With twelve amazing songs composed and arranged by a wide range of jewish music’s
finest talent, there is something in this album for everyone!
I hope you enjoy! Moshe
Thank you
Mendy Hershkowitz
Where do I begin? From the first time we
sat down together, throughout this incredi-
Firstly, I want to thank Hashem, ble journey, your unparalleled guidance and
for everything He’s done for me and giving creativity made this unique album. Thanks
me the Siyata D’shmaya to make this for doing what you do and making this a
album a dream come true. true masterpiece!

Thank you for being my biggest supporter in Yeshiva, who would have thought we

happened without you! talent is beyond description. I couldn’t have
done this without you!

to be better!

«Worship G-d with joy» - a verse from Tehillim that became the founda-
tion of Chassidus.
The song and the nigun are an inseparable part of the Chassidic avoda –
service. The nigun, it is said, uplifts and inspires man .
Rabbi Hillel of Paritch, one of the greatest followers of the Mitteler Rebbe
and the Rebbe Tzemach Tzedek, once said: «One who has a feel for ne-
gina, has a better feeling of Chassidus.»
While visiting at various Chabad Houses in venues at far-flung corners
of the earth, on many varied and important occasions, it became ob-
vious,when singing the Seder Nigunim of our Rebbeim, that there is a
lack of clarity among many regarding the correct order and nigun of each
of the Rebbeim. I wondered how I could convey the emotive feelings of
yearning and longing that the Seder Nigunim brings forth. How could I
make the proper order and background of each nigun of the Seder Nigunim
accessible to all, internalizing and integrating this sublime Seder, which is
also sung in shuls of Anash at the Seudas Moshiach and conclusion of the
Shovuos holiday?
After tremendous spiritual and material effort and investment, I am very
pleased to present to you The Nigunim Project.
This project, unlike its predecessors, was produced and recorded in a very
unique way. Every note, every movement, was recorded once, twice or even
three times, until we reached the finished product, which is absolutely
accurate, to our professional and musical satisfaction, in a meticulous
and painstaking manner, in order to preserve the original nigun as it
ימלועה תופיטעה ןויכרא
pdfcd17@gmail.com
Hanigunim Booklet.indd 2 21/11/2017 16:35
j.music booklet archive
pdfcd17@gmail.com
was transmitted from previous generations to this generation, and to
generations to come.
The Nigunim presented on this unique album are the very nigunim that the
Rebbe ordered to be sung during the designated times, at the Farbrengens
in 770.
As a addition, we also included the arrangement of the Alter Rebbe’s Nigun,
the nigun of Arba Bavos followed by Nye Zhuritze Chloptzi, in accordance
with the order sung by the Rebbe, so that they will be sung properly.
Heartfelt thanks to R’ Sheya Segal of Kehot Publishing House - for
his guidance and attention to every detail throughout the recording and
singing stages of the project, as well as to R’ Shimi Segal, R’ Simcha
Friedman and R’ Mordechai Brodsky.
To Moshe Laufer for his dedicated work, and the outstanding result -
the magnificent arrangements which intensify the vibrancy and joy of the
nigunim. The nigunim, firmly rooted in all that is holy and sublime, coupled
with innovative sound and state of the art technology, while preserving the
fiery authentic tradition, brought the project to fruition .
Many thanks to the talented vocalists: R’ Avraham Fried, R’ Yoni Shlomo,
R’ Simcha Friedman, Rabbi Eli Marcus, R. Berel Zucker and R’ Shmil.
B’Birkas Chassidim,
Moshe Dov

ת”יהזעב
.תודיסחה דוסי תויהל ךפהש םילהתמ קוספ – ”החמשב ’ה תא ודבע“
םמורמ – ורמא ךכ – ןוגינה .תידיסחה ’ה תדובעמ דרפנ יתלב קלח אוה ןוגינהו רישה
.םדאה תא ררועמו
אטבתה ,קדצ-חמצהו יעצמאה ר”ומדא לש םהידיסח ילודגמ דחא ,שטיראפמ ללה יבר
.״תודיסחב רתוי בוט שוח ול שי – הניגנב שוח ול שיש ימ“ :רמאו םעפ
* * *
ןמז עיגהשכ ,תובושחו תונוש תויונמדזהב ,םלועה הצקב םש יא ד״בח יתבב בשוי ידועב

Mitteler
Rebbe’s
Nigun
The
Kapelye
j.music booklet archive
pdfcd17@gmail.com
Originally, in Yiddish, Der Mitteler Rebbe’s Kapelye), a nigun
composed by the Mittler Rebbe’s choir. The nigun was endeared by
the Rebbe.
The Kapelye was comprised of two divisions: vocalists, and musi-
cians. When they played on weekdays, it was similar to the service
of the Leviim in the Bais Hamikdosh, who sang during the sacrificing
of the public and obligatory sacrifices that were offered at the Bais
HaMikdosh.
The main function of the choir was to sing and play in the Mitteler
Rebbe’s presence, when he appeared in public- while lighting
Hanukkah candles, going out in the field on Lag BaOmer and more.
The Mitteler Rebbe was very fond of the choir and was involved in
managing its affairs through various instructions regarding their
singing. Several times, the Rebbe carried out spiritual acts through
the Kapelye. .
Other melodies composed by members of the choir are the nigun for
Birkas Kohanim and for the .blessing of the Kohanim and the nigun
Nye Zuritze Chluptzi .

Rebbe
Rayatz’s
Nigun
The
“Beinoni”
j.music booklet archive
pdfcd17@gmail.com
The nigun was composed by the Chossid Rabbi Aharon Charitonov of
the city of Nikolaev. This melody was considered to be the nigun of
the Rebbe Rayatz, who was particularly fond of it, and even gave the
nigun its name, «The Beinoni,» saying that this nigun corresponds to,
and reflects the struggle of the Beinoni, as is explained in the Tanya.
The connection of the nigun to the Redemption :
On the night of Simchas Torah, 5749, the Rebbe related to the Seder
Nigunim of all the Rebbeim, and spoke of the nigun of the Rebbe
Rayatz, known as the “Beinoni”, in connection with preparation
for the Geulah:
«It is necessary to comment on the nigun of the Rebbe, my father-
in-law, which he called the» Beinoni- connected with the coming of
Moshiach Tzidkenu, because» our actions and work « that bring the
Geulah (beginning with the psak din (ruling) of the Rambam that ‘one
mitzvah can bring favorable judgment for him and the entire world,
and effected for him and them, deliverance and salvation) «Most of
the Torah is attributed to) ( as the Torah speaks to the majority) the
avodah (service) of the Beinoni which is» from the character of every
person, after which every person will draw, «and therefore the work
of the righteous who are few.

A spirited nigun sung by Chazzan Rabbi Yechiel Halperin in Lubavitch in the presence of
the Rebbe Rashab and the Rebbe Rayatz.
The words of the melody, expressing hope and confidence that the redemption is near, are
part of the Shabbos and Yom Tov Mussaf prayers.
The Rebbe taught this nigun on Simchas Torah 5724, before daybreak.
Before the Rebbe began to teach the nigun, he asked for a Siddur, and began to teach the
nigun with the Siddur open before him. He sang the end of the nigun without words.
After he finished singing once, he said, «This melody was sung in Rostov and Lubavitch
before the Rebbe, and seemingly even on Rosh Hashana during the “Kesser” prayer,
before the Rebbe.
When they finished singing, the Rebbe turned to R’ Reuven Dunin, who was present, and
bid him sing the nigun again. R’ Reuven had not yet mastered the nigun, and the Rebbe
said: «In this nigun the central focus is the text- words that express our confidence in
redemption, and not the tune!»
The next day, during the Mussaf prayer, the new melody was sung in «Kesser», which the
Rebbe began in a very high tone, almost shouting, «Hu Elokeinu».
Hu Elokeinu was the last nigun taught by the Rebbe.
